_meta.methodology: BUILD_PROCESS.md; P0.7 zero-debt (follow-up flagged in FR-0008)

title: Dedupe perf: make findDuplicates O(n) (hash-indexed) instead of O(n^2)
business_reason: findDuplicates compares each incoming row against every existing AND every accepted-unique row (existing.some + unique.some) — O(I*(E+U)). A 5000-row import validate took ~6s; it degrades quadratically and would dominate large imports (the >=5000 approval path). Pure-function refactor to O(n) via hash sets; identical results.

drift_findings_step3.current: server/crm/dedupe.ts findDuplicates: nested .some() scans -> O(n^2). isDuplicate matches on any of email / phone-tail(>=10) / website / (normalized_school_name + city|state) key.
drift_findings_step3.callers: createLead (duplicate_warning), importLeads stage 1 (dedupe). isDuplicate is also exercised by workflow.test.ts 'CRM duplicate detection' — semantics must stay identical.

param_14.question: Make duplicate detection scale to large imports without changing which rows are flagged.
param_14.answer: Build four hash Sets (norm(email), phone-tail, norm(website), leadKey) from existing, then for each incoming O(1) lookup across the same dimensions; accepted-unique rows are indexed incrementally so within-batch duplicates still match. isDuplicate/leadKey kept and unchanged; findDuplicates signature + result order preserved.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: createLead + importLeads consume findDuplicates unchanged; workflow.test.ts must stay green (parity)
param_14.assumptions: Match semantics are exactly the OR-of-dimensions in isDuplicate; result is the same set/order as before.

principles_pass.behavior_parity: P4.6 — a parity test asserts identical unique/duplicate partitioning vs the documented match rules; workflow.test.ts unchanged + green
principles_pass.perf: O(I+E) with O(1) set lookups; a large-input test (20k rows) that would time out under O(n^2) completes fast
principles_pass.no_api_change: P0.6 — same exported signature; callers untouched; no schema/migration

functional: email/phone/website/name+city key each flag a duplicate
perf: 20,000 incoming rows dedupe completes quickly + correct counts (would not terminate timely under O(n^2))
